gold.title: Timeline
gold.reason: 9,313 downloads and 140 likes — highest engagement in the folder. Scroll-driven animated timeline with sticky header and progress beam is directly applicable to SaaS feature pages, product roadmaps, and onboarding flows. Framer Motion integration is clean and production-quality.

gold.title: Marquee
gold.reason: Highly versatile infinite-scroll marquee used on virtually every modern SaaS landing page for social proof, logo strips, and feature highlights. Clean, dependency-light implementation with vertical/horizontal support and pauseOnHover.

gold.title: Sticky Scroll Reveal
gold.reason: 1,315 downloads. Sticky container with scroll-linked text reveal is a signature pattern on premium SaaS sites (Linear, Stripe, etc.). Polished Framer Motion implementation makes it drop-in ready for feature explanation sections.

gold.title: Dialog
gold.reason: 8,969 downloads and 101 likes — second-highest engagement overall. Enhanced Radix UI dialog with refined styling is an essential primitive for any production website or SaaS product. Universally applicable and high design quality.

gold.title: Marquee Effect
gold.reason: 550 downloads, 12 likes. Scroll-velocity-aware marquee with Framer Motion physics creates an impressive, tactile feel. Ideal for hero sections and social-proof strips on SaaS and portfolio sites.

gold.title: Scroll Velocity
gold.reason: 682 downloads, 12 likes. Velocity-responsive infinite horizontal scroll supporting both images and text is a modern, eye-catching pattern. Dark mode compatible and responsive — excellent for logo carousels and brand showcases.

silver.title: Tabs
silver.reason: 2,595 downloads, 20 likes. Enhanced Radix UI tabs are broadly useful for any website, but tabs as a standalone primitive are less visually distinctive than marquees or timelines for a redesign tool.

silver.title: Text Gradient Scroll
silver.reason: 481 downloads, 10 likes. Scroll-triggered opacity gradient per word/letter adds a premium feel to long-form content sections. Useful for editorial and marketing pages, but narrower use case than a full marquee or timeline.

silver.title: Scroll Progress
silver.reason: 211 downloads, 9 likes. Animated scroll progress indicator with spring physics is a polished UX touch for blog posts and long-form pages. Clean implementation but serves a narrow, supplementary role.

silver.title: Scroll and Swap Text
silver.reason: Good concept — vertically swapping letters on scroll is distinctive for hero headings. Lower download count (142) but the visual effect is sophisticated and applicable to portfolio or agency sites.

silver.title: Modern Timeline
silver.reason: Supports vertical/horizontal layouts, animated progress, dark/light mode, and rich content. Excellent for SaaS roadmaps and onboarding, though lower adoption (54 downloads) suggests it is newer or less discovered.

silver.title: Horizontal Scroll Carousel
silver.reason: 295 downloads. Scroll-driven horizontal image carousel is visually engaging for portfolio and e-commerce sites. Next.js Image dependency makes it slightly less portable as a generic web component.

silver.title: Sticky Section Tabs
silver.reason: Useful pattern for long content pages with sticky section headers. Solid concept for documentation or feature-comparison pages, but implementation is minimal and download count is low (235).

silver.title: Sticky Scroll
silver.reason: Uses Lenis smooth-scroll library for a premium native-scroll feel. Good for agency and portfolio sites that need polished scroll behavior, but zero downloads and no description reduce confidence.

silver.title: Scroll Based Velocity
silver.reason: 344 downloads. Velocity-responsive scrolling text is a compelling hero effect, but functionally overlaps with Edil-ozi__scroll-velocity. Good quality but more niche as a standalone effect.

bronze.title: Scroll Button
bronze.reason: Floating button to return to the bottom of a container is too narrow and situational. Low downloads (42) confirm limited applicability for general website redesign.

bronze.title: XScroll
bronze.reason: A thin wrapper around Radix ScrollArea adding only horizontal support. Useful as a utility but too simple and low-level to qualify as a marketing block for visual redesign.

bronze.title: Scroll Area
bronze.reason: CVA-styled scroll area with orientation variants. Useful as a UI primitive but not a marketing block — no visual personality and only 12 downloads.

bronze.title: Scroll Area
bronze.reason: Thin wrapper around Radix ScrollArea. Minimal differentiation from the standard shadcn component; 19 downloads confirms limited unique value.

bronze.title: Scroll Area
bronze.reason: Demo scroll area listing version tags. Essentially a usage example of shadcn ScrollArea with no original design. Only 3 downloads.

bronze.title: Scroll Area
bronze.reason: Basic Base UI scroll area demo. Purely functional with no visual design contribution. Only 2 downloads.

bronze.title: Scroll-linked Spring Animation
bronze.reason: Simple scroll progress bar using spring physics. Minimal implementation with 3 downloads — more of a proof-of-concept than a production-ready block.

bronze.title: Desert Drift
bronze.reason: An iframe embedding a car-drifting scroll animation. Highly experimental and domain-specific — not applicable to general business or SaaS website redesign.
